title: Victims of San Diego Mosque Shooting Remembered as Heroes and Leaders
description: Muslim groups identified the three men killed in the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego.
author: Christina Morales, Candice Reed and Neil Vigdor

title: Trump Endorses Ken Paxton in Texas Senate Runoff Race Against John Cornyn
description: The president embraced Ken Paxton, a MAGA loyalist, over Senator John Cornyn, despite warnings from Republican leaders about Mr. Paxton’s history of scandal.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: Who Are the Suspects in the San Diego Mosque Shooting?
description: Authorities identified the teenagers they say killed three men, and then themselves, in an attack on the largest mosque in the county.
author: Tim Arango and Chelsia Rose Marcius

title: I.R.S. Prohibited From Pursuing Audits of Trump and His Family
description: As part of the Justice Department’s compensation fund deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.
author: Alan Feuer and Andrew Duehren

title: Polls in Kentucky’s 4th District Show an Extremely Tight Race
description: With support from President Trump, Ed Gallrein is challenging Representative Thomas Massie.
author: Caroline Soler

title: Republican Senators Are Livid at Trump’s Endorsement of Paxton
description: News that President Trump had snubbed Senator John Cornyn of Texas, the four-term incumbent, was met with shock, anger and fear that the G.O.P. could lose his seat.
author: Michael Gold, Megan Mineiro and Carl Hulse

title: Who is Ken Paxton, Trump’s pick in the Texas Senate race?
description: Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, seems poised to defeat Senator John Cornyn, a four-term incumbent.
author: Michael Kruse and Lauren McGaughy

title: Pentagon Inspector General to Assess U.S. Boat Strikes
description: The inspector general’s office said it would evaluate whether U.S. Southern Command followed a six-part process for carrying out airstrikes.
author: John Ismay

title: Brad Raffensperger Defends Embattled Georgia Election Workers
description: Brad Raffensperger, state’s top election official, is hoping to qualify for a runoff in the Republican primary for governor.
author: Johnny Kauffman

title: The N.A.A.C.P. calls on Black athletes to boycott Southern states after redistricting.
author: Emily Cochrane

title: Sean Duffy to Testify Amid Questions About Road Trip YouTube Series
description: The transportation secretary’s reality-show-like series is meant to commemorate the nation’s 250th birthday but has faced questions about its funding.
author: Karoun Demirjian

title: Manager at Trump’s N.J. Golf Club Helped Plan Reflecting Pool Repairs
description: David Schutzenhofer, a Trump Organization employee, reached out to one of the vendors the government later hired for the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial, documents show.
author: David A. Fahrenthold and Maxine Joselow

title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Shatters a Neighborhood’s Peace
description: First came a series of pops, then a race to render aid. It was too late.
author: Candice Reed

title: As Trump weighs in on primaries, his approval rating has sunk to a second-term low.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: Abortion Pill Lawsuit Leaves Trump in a Political Bind Ahead of the Midterms
description: Louisiana wants the Food and Drug Administration to curtail access to the medication. Doing so could cost Republicans at the polls.
author: Pam Belluck and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

title: Dallas World Cup Mural: Artist Wyland Says Beloved Whale Artwork Was Painted Over
description: The artwork’s removal ahead of the World Cup has ignited a backlash over public art and what cities are willing to sacrifice for global events.
author: Jesus Jiménez

title: College Republicans Split Over Whether to Embrace the Far Right
description: As some college Republicans invite white nationalists into their organizations, other young conservatives have recoiled. The divide could affect upcoming elections.
author: Stephanie Saul

title: Iran War Exposes Shortcomings in U.S. Military Industrial Base
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is taking on a problem that the Pentagon and Congress have tried, and mostly failed, to address for years.
author: Julian E. Barnes

title: How Trump Entrusted His World Cup to Another Giuliani
description: Andrew Giuliani, the son of Rudy and a longtime Trump loyalist, is overseeing American hosting duties for the World Cup next month. It’s a big promotion.
author: Matt Flegenheimer and Rebecca R. Ruiz

title: A Single Donation Could Upend the G.O.P. Primary for Texas Attorney General
description: One donor’s contribution of $2.75 million could help propel Representative Chip Roy in his clash with a self-funded state senator, Mayes Middleton.
author: Lauren McGaughy
